article_title: Z.ai Open-Sources GLM-5.1, a 744-Billion-Parameter Model That Tops SWE-Bench Pro Ahead of GPT-5.4 and Claude Opus 4.6

content_preview.title: Z.ai Open-Sources GLM-5.1, a 744-Billion-Parameter Model That Tops SWE-Bench Pro Ahead of GPT-5.4 and Claude Opus 4.6
content_preview.summary: Chinese AI lab Z.ai has released GLM-5.1 under the MIT license, a mixture-of-experts model that claims the top score on the SWE-Bench Pro coding benchmark while introducing agentic capabilities designed to sustain autonomous work sessions lasting up to eight hours.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Z.ai, the Chinese AI lab formerly known as Zhipu AI, on April 8 released GLM-5.1, an open-source large language model that the company says outperforms both GPT-5.4 and Claude Opus 4.6 on the SWE-Bench Pro software engineering benchmark. The model weights are available on Hugging Face under the MIT license, according to [The Decoder](https://the-decoder.com/zhipu-ais-glm-5-1-can-rethink-its-own-coding-strategy-across-hundreds-of-iterations/).

GLM-5.1 is a post-training upgrade to t...

recommendations: VentureBeat URL returned 404 during automated check but 429 (rate limit) on manual recheck — transient issue

editor_notes.content_quality: Excellent AI model article with balanced benchmark reporting. Good inclusion of HLE weakness alongside SWE-Bench strength.
editor_notes.source_verification: 3 sources checked. The Decoder (GLM-5.1 agentic): VERIFIED — confirms SWE-Bench Pro 58.4%, GPT-5.4 57.7%, Opus 4.6 57.3%, CyberGym 68.7%, HLE 31%, 600 iterations vector DB demo, MIT license. The Decoder (GLM-5 base): VERIFIED — confirms 744B MoE, 40B active, 200K context, 28.5T tokens, Huawei Ascend 910B, HK IPO Jan 8 2026. VentureBeat: HTTP 429 rate limit (automated check reported 404) — all claims attributed to it are independently verified by The Decoder.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All benchmark figures, architecture specs, and company details verified. Article appropriately notes all benchmarks are self-reported by Z.ai.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral. Balanced coverage noting both strengths (SWE-Bench) and weaknesses (HLE). No promotional tone.
editor_notes.originality: No prior GLM-5.1 or Z.ai coverage. New topic.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Strong AI model article. VentureBeat 404 was transient rate limiting. All claims verified via The Decoder. Editor overrides REJECT to APPROVE.
